title: Write decimals and fractions greater than 1 shown on number lines
description: Practice writing decimal numbers shown in grid diagrams.
topic_path: Write decimals and fractions greater than 1 shown on number lines

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 3 to 5, with 10 equal sections between each whole number. A point labeled question mark is 2 sections to the right of 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/01eecd10a2437a5feb459a41219b6f8037e9973c)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a fraction and a decimal.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are $10$ equal distances between each two whole numbers on the number line.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{ one tenth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{ \text{2 tenths}}$ after $\purpleE{4}$.

![The same number line as above showing that 4 plus 2 tenths equals 4.2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b2081c48ec43e549eb0517077cb0246c40a630a7)

We can write the fraction as $\purpleE{4}\goldD{\dfrac{2}{10}}$ or $\blueD{\dfrac{42}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\purpleE{4 \text{ ones}}+\goldD{2 \text{ tenths}} $.

 $\purpleE{4}$ and $\goldD{ \text{2 tenths}}$ equals $\blueD{4.2}$. 

Ones | $.$ |  Tenths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$\purpleE{4}$ | $.$ | $\goldD{2}$

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{42}{10}$

* Decimal: $4.2$

:-: | :-: |:-: |:-:

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{87}{10}$

* Decimal: $8.7$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 2 to 4, with 10 equal sections between each whole number. A point labeled question mark is 6 sections to the right of 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/73c88b810fdd767948bf5201e82fd32d9012cfcf)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a mixed number and a decimal.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are $10$ equal distances between each two whole numbers on the number line.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{ one tenth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{ \text{6 tenths}}$ after $\purpleD{3}$.

![The same number line as above showing that 3 plus 6 tenths equals 3.6](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a6514ba75a854796107b2493f5bf84584b7e7555)

We can write the fraction as $\purpleD{3}\goldD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$ or $\blueD{\dfrac{36}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\purpleD{3 \text{ ones}}+\goldD{6 \text{ tenths}} $.

 $\purpleD{3}$ and $\goldD{ \text{6 tenths}}$ equals $\blueD{3.6}$. 

Ones | $.$ |  Tenths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$\purpleD{3}$ | $.$ | $\goldD{6}$

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Mixed number:  $3\dfrac{6}{10}$

* Decimal: $3.6$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 7 to 9, with 10 equal sections between each whole number. A point labeled question mark is 1 section to the right of 7.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3592c03b2c4f3cdbe287709a073da3cc9ca76f62)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a mixed number and a decimal.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are $10$ equal distances between each two whole numbers on the number line.
Each tick mark represents a length of $\goldD{0.1}$, or $\goldD{\text{ one tenth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{ \text{1 tenth}}$ after $\purpleD{7}$.

![The same number line as above showing that 7 plus 1 tenth equals 7.1](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f27036ec3b14237c1f1e179980dc4c0d2cb50ec5)

We can write the fraction as $\purpleD{7}\goldD{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ or $\blueD{\dfrac{71}{10}}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can use a place value chart to help us write $\purpleD{7 \text{ ones}}+\goldD{1 \text{ tenth}} $.

 $\purpleD{7}$ and $\goldD{ \text{1 tenth}}$ equals $\blueD{7.1}$. 

Ones | $.$ |  Tenths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$\purpleD{7}$ | $.$ | $\goldD{1}$

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Mixed number:  $7\dfrac{1}{10}$

* Decimal: $7.1$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 8.6 to 8.8, with 10 equal sections between each whole tenth. A point labeled question mark is 1 section to the right of 8.6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/99b01630cc516a6735999590cf87cfad53a9cc68)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a fraction and a decimal.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The length between the labeled tick marks is $\blueD{0.1}$, or $\blueD{1 \text{ tenth}}$.
There are $10$ equal distances between each $\blueD{0.1}$ on the number line. Those distances are each $\goldD{0.01}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{ \text{1 hundredth}}$ after $\purpleD{8.6}$.

![A portion of the number line above, from 8.6 to 8.7 showing that the point is 1 hundredth to the right of 8.6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f44072cee7344787d6f672a7e1d1f05629fb984e)

$8.6$ and $\goldD{ \text{1 hundredth}}$ equals ${8.6\goldD1}$. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$8$ | $.$ | $6$  | $\goldD{1}$

We can read the number as $8$ and $61$ hundredths.

We can write the fraction as $8\dfrac{61}{100}$ or $\dfrac{861}{100}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{861}{100}$

* Decimal: $8.61$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 7.4 to 7.6, with 10 equal sections between each whole tenth. A point labeled question mark is 6 sections to the right of 7.4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c2666d513362f2f18c49fd4a53c0ebf4d1991d2)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a fraction and a decimal.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The length between the labeled tick marks is $\tealD{0.1}$, or $\tealD{1 \text{ tenth}}$.
There are $10$ equal distances between each $\tealD{0.1}$ on the number line. Those distances are each $\goldD{0.01}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{ \text{6 hundredths}}$ after $\purpleD{7.4}$.

![A portion of the number line above, from 7.4 to 7.5 showing that the point is 6 hundredths to the right of 7.4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0f6dae04f5ff65dea349148f011cb9d0da1a46c)

$7.4$ and $\goldD{ \text{6 hundredths}}$ equals ${7.4\goldD{6}}$. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$7$ | $.$ | $4$  | $\goldD{6}$

We can read the number as $7$ and $46$ hundredths.

We can write the fraction as $7\dfrac{46}{100}$ or $\dfrac{746}{100}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{746}{100}$

* Decimal: $7.46$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 4 to 4.2, with 10 equal sections between each whole tenth. A point labeled question mark is 3 sections to the right of 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/40c4aedd4c41a583d1b5433c7ef40d28058c5f25)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a mixed number and a decimal.**

hints: The length between the labeled tick marks is $\tealD{0.1}$, or $\tealD{1 \text{ tenth}}$.
There are $10$ equal distances between each $\tealD{0.1}$ on the number line. Those distances are each $\goldD{0.01}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

$\purpleD{4}$ and $\goldD{ \text{3 hundredths}}$ equals $\purpleD{4}.0\goldD{3}$. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$4$ | $.$ | $0$  | $\goldD{3}$

We can read the number as $4$ and $3$ hundredths.

We can write the fraction as $4\dfrac{3}{100}$ or $\dfrac{403}{100}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Mixed number:  $4\dfrac{3}{100}$

* Decimal: $4.03$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

![A number line from 1 to 1.2, with 10 equal sections between each whole tenth. A point labeled question mark is 9 sections to the right of 1.1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f26ef377a8baae450c716f0115102a64f1cd0cdf)

**Describe the location of the point on the number line with a mixed number and a decimal.**

hints: The length between the labeled tick marks is $\tealD{0.1}$, or $\tealD{1 \text{ tenth}}$.
There are $10$ equal distances between each $\tealD{0.1}$ on the number line. Those distances are each $\goldD{0.01}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{ \text{9 hundredths}}$ after $\purpleD{1.1}$.

![A portion of the number line above, from 1.1 to 1.2 showing that the point is 9 hundredths to the right of 1.1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9541905b2df2d28e192042aa99eff5785f41dd8f)

$\purpleD{1.1}$ and $\goldD{ \text{9 hundredths}}$ equals $\purpleD{1.1}\goldD{9}$. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$1$ | $.$ | $1$  | $\goldD{9}$

We can read the number as $1$ and $19$ hundredths.

We can write the fraction as $1\dfrac{19}{100}$ or $\dfrac{119}{100}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Mixed number:  $1\dfrac{19}{100}$

* Decimal: $1.19$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Identify the point on the number line and write it as both a fraction and a decimal.**

**Fraction:** [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
 
**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
On the number line, the length between each whole number is split into ten equal pieces. 

Each of these pieces has a length of $\tealE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ .

[[☃ image 1]]

Starting at $0$, we can move $\tealE{14}$ lengths of $\tealE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ to get to the point.


[[☃ image 1]]

The point is located at $\tealE{\dfrac{14}{10}}$.

Starting at $0$, we can also move $\maroonE{1 \text{ one}}$ and $\tealE{4 \text{ tenths}}$ to get to the point.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can use a place value chart to write $\maroonE{1 \text{ one}}$ and $\tealE{4 \text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\maroonE{1}$|$.$  | $\tealE4$ |
The point on the number line represents the fraction $\dfrac{14}{10}$ and the decimal $1.4$.

options.alt: A number line labeled from 0 to 2 with tick marks every one-tenth unit. Tick marks 0, 1, and 2 are labeled. There is a dot at the fourth tick mark to the right of 1.
